channel.title: briefUpdatesHealthOffshootNL
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from briefUpdatesHealthOffshootNL, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: This Ballad Hospital, Flooded by Hurricane Helene, Will Be Rebuilt for $44M in a Flood Plain
item.description: Ballad Health, the nation’s largest state-sanctioned hospital monopoly, plans to rebuild Unicoi County Hospital on land that two climate modeling comp...

item.title: Public Health Workers Are Quitting Over Assignments to Guantánamo
item.description: U.S. Public Health Service doctors and nurses are being deployed to Guantánamo and other detention centers as President Donald Trump escalates mass ar...

item.title: March for Life attendees may have been exposed to measles, DC Health warns
item.description: D.C. health officials are contacting people possibly exposed to measles at the March for Life in January, as confirmed cases rise nationwide.

item.title: Olympic COVID restrictions are gone, but some athletes are still self-quarantining
item.description: For most people, the pandemic days of masking are behind them. In certain corners of the Winter Olympics, though, things still look a lot like they di...

item.title: Need a new path in midlife? There's a school for that and a quiz to kickstart it
item.description: Schools across the country are offering courses and retreats for people 50+ who want to reinvent themselves and embrace lifelong learning and discover...

item.title: New study finds many tuberculosis diagnoses may be incorrect
item.description: New research is now casting doubt on the true number of tuberculosis cases. That could carry significant implications for patient care and well-being.

item.title: Watch: Is MAHA the New MAGA?
item.description: With fractures emerging in the Make America Great Again movement, some Republicans are looking to capitalize on its “MAHA” counterpart ahead of the mi...

item.title: Trump administration lauds plastic surgeons' statement on trans surgery for minors
item.description: A patient who came to regret the top surgery she got as a teen won a $2 million malpractice suit. Then, the American Society of Plastic Surgeons clari...

item.title: Journalists Describe Drivers of High Health Costs and Spell Out the Science of Protein
item.description: KFF Health News journalists made the rounds on national and local media recently to discuss topical stories. Here’s a collection of their appearances.

item.title: Newsom Tries To Thread Needle on Immigrant Health as Ambitions Turn National
item.description: Get our weekly newsletter, The Week in Brief, featuring a roundup of our original coverage, Fridays at 2 p.m. ET.

item.title: Will HHS Enhance or Stall the Promise of Artificial Intelligence for Healthcare?
item.description: By STEVEN ZECOLA In its Strategy for Artificial Intelligence (V.3), the Department of Health and Human Services (“HHS”) acknowledges that: “For too lo...

item.title: Arnold Ventures Part II “Structuring Information Felicitously”
item.description: By JEFF GOLDSMITH In the first part of our look at Arnold Ventures, we explored its business model and generous support of elite University health pol...

item.title: Primary care, Concierge Care & AI – How to fix health care
item.description: At last week’s HITLAB January 2026 Symposium, Matthew Holt gave a talk about what’s wrong with primary care, how concierge care for all can fix it, an...

item.title: John Arnold: The Most Powerful Man in Healthcare Nobody has Ever Heard Of (Pt I)
item.description: By JEFF GOLDSMITH It has happened at least a dozen times. I mention John Arnold and am greeted by knowledgeable healthcare colleagues with a blank sta...

item.title: Shifting Sands Part 3
item.description: By GEORGE BEAUREGARD Fifteen months ago, I wrote in The Health Care Blog about the “incoming tide” of early-onset cancer. At that time, the global ris...
